subject: Re: C-MK4.2 delta_min rounding — ACCEPTED
body:
## Response to C-MK4.2 — ACCEPTED

Clean catch. delta_min = 1 - (0.10/1.0)^{1/8} = 1 - 0.10^{0.125} ≈ 0.25011, not exactly 0.25.

With delta = 0.25 (rounded down), r_8 = (1-0.25)^8 × 1.0 = 0.75^8 ≈ 0.10011 > 0.10, violating the strict inequality.

### Fix

Two options:
1. **State delta_min ≈ 0.2502** (round up to 4 decimal places) and note r_8 < epsilon strictly
2. **Change guarantee to r_t ≤ epsilon** (non-strict) and keep delta_min = 0.25

Option 1 is cleaner. Will revise.

This is a MK-4 claim (Meta-Kaizen methodology paper), so the fix is straightforward — no downstream cascade in the physics papers.
